channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘Sirat’ representa a España en los Oscar. Su director no está seguro de qué tan española es
item.description: El cine español ha entrado en una nueva era más diversa, dicen los expertos cinematográficos. Oliver Laxe, director de ‘Sirat’, encarna este cambio.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:credit: Samuel Aranda para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El director español Oliver Laxe cuestionó “si mi cine es español o no”, y dijo que su película nominada al Oscar, Sirat, difería del trabajo de sus colegas españoles.

item.title: ‘We Had Flunked Out of College and Did Not Want to Face Our Parents’
item.description: Traveling from Cleveland by thumb, a wallet surfaces in Brooklyn and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: A Refugee Died After Border Patrol Left Him at a Cafe. Fear Followed.
item.description: Buffalo’s Arakan Rohingya community was rattled after a disabled man’s death. “Our worry comes from future incidents that may happen,” one resident said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sommer

media:content.media:credit: Craig Ruttle/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The body of Nurul Amin Shah Alam was prepared for burial on Feb. 26 in Buffalo. He was found dead five days after Border Patrol agents dropped him at a doughnut shop.

item.title: She Was Paralyzed by a Subway Train. Today, She’s Reclaiming Her Life.
item.description: The artist Emine Yilmaz was shoved into a passing subway car three years ago. Six surgeries later, she’s drawing again.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko and Jonah Markowitz

media:content.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times

item.title: Commuters, Rejoice! The New Portal Bridge in New Jersey Is Ready for Riders.
item.description: The old Portal Bridge disrupted rail commutes in New Jersey for decades, frequently getting stuck when it swung to let boat traffic pass. Now a new bridge is open to the public.
item.dc:creator: Michael Harmon

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Portal North Bridge’s arches were built at the Port of Coeymans, near Albany, then floated down the Hudson River and up the Hackensack River to the job site.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 15, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Two Arrested in Death of Anti-Iranian Regime Activist in Canada
item.description: A man and a woman were charged with murder in what the authorities described as a “targeted incident” against Masood Masjoody, who had gone missing weeks ago in British Columbia.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: Iran Front for the Revival of Law and National Sovereignty

media:content.media:credit: Sebastien St-Jean/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Cuando los medicamentos para perder peso no funcionan
item.description: Fármacos como Wegovy y Zepbound han sido aclamados como tratamientos milagrosos. Pero una de cada 10 personas es lo que los científicos llaman “no respondedores”.
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj and Dani Blum

item.title: Trump Wins When Oscar Winners Stay Silent
item.description: Right now it does matter a lot whether actors can find the right words.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Kehlmann

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by Shannon Lin/The New York Times

item.title: Marseille’s Tight Mayoral Race Is a Bellwether for France’s Future
item.description: Voters across France choose mayors this week. The far right is performing strongly in the country’s second city, making the contest there a test of national shifts.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler and Ana Castelain

media:content.media:credit: James Hill for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supporters of Benoît Payan, the incumbent mayor of Marseille, France, distributing leaflets in the city on Friday. Mr. Payan is competing in a tight race featuring a strong far-right candidate.

item.title: Quote of the Day: It Came From Outer Space, and Conquered
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, March 15, 2026.

item.title: In Scolding the New U.S. Ambassador, South Africa Signals Defiance
item.description: The latest clash between the two countries illustrates the depths to which relations have sunk and the struggle to forge a new path forward.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon

media:content.media:credit: William Widmer for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Brent Bozell in New Orleans in 2019.

item.title: Iceland’s Chief ‘Lava Cooler’ Is Bracing for the Next Volcanic Eruption
item.description: Helgi Hjorleifsson, a firefighter, is a leader in a national experiment to steer rivers of lava away from important sites. Some called it crazy, but it worked.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Egill Bjarnason

media:content.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Helgi Hjorleifsson driving through lava fields in southwestern Iceland in February.

item.title: Some Olympic Leaders Want to See Fixed Winter Games Host Cities
item.description: Fewer cities are bidding for Olympics, and those that are can’t always accommodate every event. The Milan-Cortina Games were the most sprawling Olympics in history.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The International Olympic Committee has had to pivot toward flexibility as the number of bidders for its marquee winter event has started to dwindle.

item.title: Corrections: March 15, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Sunday, March 15, 2026.

item.title: Three More Iranian Soccer Players Withdraw Bid for Asylum in Australia
item.description: Seven members of the national women’s team had sought refuge in the country after they were labeled ‘traitors’ at home. Four of them have since changed their minds.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Izhar Khan/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Members of the Iranian women’s national soccer team before a match in Australia this month.

item.title: Pentagon Names 6 Military Members Killed in Iraq Tanker Crash
item.description: The crew members had been part of the U.S. and Israel’s war on Iran, bringing the death toll of American service members in the conflict to at least 13.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín and Chris Hippensteel
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Ivy Thomas/121st Air Refueling Wing Ohio Air National Guard, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Tech. Sgt. Tyler H. Simmons aboard a KC-135 Stratotanker in 2023, during a flight at Rickenbacker Air National Guard Base in Ohio. Sergeant Simmons was one of the six crew members killed when the refueling plane they were aboard crashed in Iraq.

item.title: For Trump, a Promised Economic Boom Collides With the Costs of War
item.description: President Trump had envisioned a growing economy and improving fortunes for American families in 2026. That appears at risk in his war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Colby Smith

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: By declaring war on Iran, the president has upended his vision for the nation’s economic trajectory.

item.title: F.C.C. Chair Threatens to Revoke Broadcasters’ Licenses Over War Coverage
item.description: The comment from Brendan Carr came on the heels of a social media message from President Trump criticizing the news media’s coverage of the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Brendan Carr in Washington in January.

item.title: Fund-raising Email Features Trump at Ritual for Soldiers Killed in Iran War
item.description: The email from the group Never Surrender seeks donations for President Trump from those who want access to “private national security briefings” from him.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mr. Trump saluting as coffins holding six Army Reservists killed by an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait were brought off a plane at Dover Air Force Base on March 7.

item.title: History Course
item.description: Miranda Kany whips up a Sunday puzzle for the ages.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: You can’t spell “lettuce” without “Et tu.”

item.title: U.S. Bombs Iranian Oil Hub
item.description: The U.S. attack on Kharg Island, Iran’s main oil export hub, came as tensions continued over control of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for oil transport.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Smoke billowing from a flare stack at the Port of Kharg Island oil terminal in Iran in 2017.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the U.S.-Israel War With Iran on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad was attacked overnight. Israeli strikes continued to hit the capitals of Iran and Lebanon, as Iran refused to relinquish control over a vital oil route.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Officials said that more than 800 people have been killed in Lebanon since the start of the war, with more than 2,100 people killed across the region.

item.title: Lily Collins’s Ring, Stolen Years Ago, Makes an Unexpected Return
item.description: The ring, which belonged to Ms. Collins, the “Emily in Paris” actress, was stolen in 2023. Joe Hakimian, a Chicago jeweler, unknowingly listed it for sale in December. Then came a surprising message.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

item.title: Michael B. Jordan es una estrella. Pero, ¿es un gran actor?
item.description: Jordan se ha ganado la taquilla y nuestros corazones. Pero hay debate sobre su oficio. Después de ver ‘Pecadores’ por cuarta vez, comprendo su talento.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris

media:content.media:credit: Warner Bros.
media:content.media:description: Michael B. Jordan plays twins in “Sinners”; he is nominated for best actor at the Academy Awards.

item.title: Sandstorm Batters Gaza, Destroying Makeshift Shelters
item.description: A sandstorm swept across the Gaza Strip on Saturday. Millions of displaced people remain there without proper shelter amid the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:credit: Dawoud Abu Alkas/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Displaced Palestinians faced a sandstorm in Gaza City on Saturday.

item.title: Abiqua Falls in Oregon Draws a State Offer After Listing on Real Estate Site
item.description: Lawmakers approved about $2 million to buy Abiqua Falls and surrounding land that had been advertised on the real estate site Redfin.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Willamette River Preservation Trust, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Abiqua Falls, near Scott Mills in northwestern Oregon. An online real estate ad said the falls “serve as the primary natural feature of the property and define both the landscape and overall character of the land.”

item.title: Dentro de una misión condenada al fracaso en Cuba
item.description: Un grupo de migrantes cubanos zarparon de los cayos de Florida y acabaron en un tiroteo frente a las costas cubanas. Eran militantes anticomunistas de grupos marginales.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei and David C. Adams

media:content.media:credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Grupos de exiliados cubanos celebraron una ceremonia en Miami el pasado fin de semana en honor de los 10 hombres que habían zarpado hacia Cuba desde Florida con un aparente alijo de armas.

item.title: Synagogue Attack Tests Bonds of Michigan Community
item.description: For more than a century, Jewish, Arab and Muslim people have lived in and around Detroit, coexisting mostly peacefully.
item.dc:creator: Dana Goldstein
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People walking toward the Shenandoah Country Club in West Bloomfield, Mich., where a Temple Israel Shabbat service was held on Friday.

item.title: Ex-State Trooper Convicted of Manslaughter in Death of Girl After Chase
item.description: Christopher Baldner, then a New York State trooper, rammed a family’s car during a high-speed chase in 2020, killing 11-year-old Monica Goods.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Michael Hill/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Christopher Baldner stood trial last fall on charges of manslaughter, second-degree murder and reckless endangerment. But those proceedings ended in a mistrial.

item.title: Two Antisemitic Attacks in Two Days in the Netherlands Shake Residents
item.description: Explosive devices were set off at a Jewish school in Amsterdam and a synagogue in Rotterdam within two days, Dutch officials said on Saturday, denouncing what they described as antisemitic attacks.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:credit: Piroschka Van De Wouw/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Police officers on Saturday outside the Amsterdam school where a blast damaged an outer wall. The mayor said Jews in Amsterdam were “increasingly confronted with antisemitism.”

item.title: Tehran Seems Largely Empty, as Many Flee and Others Weary of the Fighting
item.description: Residents say a heavy, lifeless atmosphere hangs over the streets of Iran’s capital, which has endured relentless attacks throughout the war.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Tajrish Bazaar in Tehran on Saturday. More than 1,348 civilians have been killed in Iran since the beginning of the war, according to the country’s representative to the United Nations.

item.title: Rains Inundate Hawaii, Closing Schools and Triggering Landslides
item.description: A storm system lashing Hawaii since Tuesday has caused flooding and power outages. The wettest spot in Maui had gotten nearly two feet of rain in the past 24 hours.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Maui County, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Floodwaters covering a roadway in Hana, Hawaii, on Friday. Since Tuesday, intense rains have pummeled the state, causing flooding and power outages.

item.title: How a Marine Unit in the Middle East Could Open New Phase of Iran War
item.description: The unit’s arrival in the coming days will give the Pentagon the ability to quickly launch raids.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gibbons-Neff and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Marine Expeditionary Unit during training in Okinawa, Japan, last year.

item.title: Sandstorm Batters Gaza, Slamming Makeshift Shelters
item.description: Millions of Gazans have remained without proper housing since the war, according to the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:credit: Dawoud Abu Alkas/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Displaced Palestinians faced a sandstorm in Gaza City on Saturday.

item.title: German Tourist Sought $20 Million in Damages After Troubled NYC Trip
item.description: Faycal Manz, a German tourist, claimed that a taco had given him diarrhea, a Walmart store had discriminated against him and that an interaction with the police had given him insomnia.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag

media:content.media:credit: Jabin Botsford/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The salsa from Los Tacos No. 1, a Manhattan restaurant franchise, was the subject of one of three lawsuits filed by Faycal Manz after a short trip to the United States in 2024.

item.title: Dozens of Medical Workers Killed as Israel Hits Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese health officials say that 31 health workers have been killed by Israeli strikes. Israel claimed, without evidence, that Hezbollah has been using ambulances and medical facilities.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Mohammed Zaatari/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The funeral for Youssef Assaf, a volunteer with the Lebanese Red Cross, in Tyre, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: ¿América Latina está lista para dejar ir a Cuba?
item.description: La izquierda de América Latina ha visto a la isla como su referente ideológico. Ahora, líderes de todo el espectro dudan en ayudar a una nación que está en la mira del gobierno de Trump.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El Capitolio Nacional en La Habana. El aislamiento de Cuba se profundiza en la región.

item.title: Trump Proposes New White House Visitor Screening Center
item.description: The facility would replace trailers and tents the Secret Service uses to screen visitors to the White House. It would be the latest presidential building project.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Construction of a new ballroom continued at the White House this month.

item.title: John M. Perkins Dies at 95; Evangelical Minister Espoused Social Justice
item.description: Inspired by the Gospel, he helped create a national network of community-development ministries “styled on the life of Jesus, who had the greatest concern for the weakest of people.”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Pulliam Bailey

media:content.media:credit: Lynsey Weatherspoon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mr. Perkins spoke to a crowd in 2018 at an event sponsored by his foundation in Stone Mountain, Ga.

item.title: Judge Rules Lawmaker Must Be Allowed to Join Kennedy Center Board Meeting
item.description: A judge ordered that Representative Joyce Beatty, a Democrat, be given access to documents and have the chance to oppose changes to the center at next week’s board meeting.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts now features President Trump’s name on its exterior.

item.title: Jürgen Habermas Dies at 96; One of Postwar Germany’s Most Influential Thinkers
item.description: In dozens of books, he rejected postmodern cynicism about truth and reason, arguing that rational communication was the best way to redeem democratic society.
item.dc:creator: Gal Beckerman

media:content.media:credit: Sueddeutsche Zeitung Photo/Alamy Stock Photo
media:content.media:description: Jurgen Habermas at the University of Frankfurt in 1969. He was best known for introducing the idea of a “public sphere” in the early 1960s.

item.title: Is There Method to Trump’s Madness?
item.description: Don’t hold your breath.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Damon Winter/The New York Times

item.title: How to Make Wolfgang Puck’s Chicken Potpie
item.description: When eaten on the couch in your pajamas, it makes for a very nice Oscars night.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Wolfgang Puck’s chicken potpie, adapted by The New York Times.

item.title: Trump Administration Approves Ultra-Deepwater Oil Drilling Plan
item.description: The $5 billion project in the Gulf of Mexico is expected to help produce up to 10 billion barrels of oil by the end of this decade. Critics say it could endanger people and marine life.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: Bureau of Ocean Energy Management, Regulation and Enforcement

media:content.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An oil platform in the Gulf of Mexico in 2024.

item.title: Trump Pardons: Lobbyist Charged in Extortion Attempt
item.description: Josh Nass had recently represented Joseph Schwartz, a former nursing home executive who was convicted of tax crimes before being freed by the president late last year.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel, William K. Rashbaum and Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Travis Dove for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Josh Nass, a lawyer and lobbyist who recently represented a former nursing home executive seeking a pardon, was arrested on an extortion charge on Friday.

item.title: Lebanon’s Displaced Flee Israeli Strikes One Year Into War
item.description: Israeli calls for the evacuation of huge swaths of southern Lebanon have led 830,000 people, about 14 percent of Lebanon’s population, to flee.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A man lighting a fire to keep warm along Beirut’s waterfront, after fleeing the city’s southern suburbs last week.

item.title: U.S. Tells Citizens to Leave Iraq After Second Attack on Embassy
item.description: An Iran-backed militia took responsibility for an overnight attack on the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad. President Trump called on other nations to help escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Ohad Zwigenberg/Associated Press

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
